<<I am>>  
The function needs to process a list of tuples, which may contain nested structures. It should iterate through each element of the tuples, check if it is numeric, and sum only the numeric values. The function must handle nested tuples by recursively exploring their contents. It should ignore any non-numeric entities like strings, booleans, or other data types. The output should be a single integer representing the total sum of all numeric values in the input structure.  

******  

<<at home>>  
The function must be designed to handle complex nested tuples, ensuring that all levels of the structure are traversed. It should use a recursive approach to navigate through the tuples and their sub-tuples. The function should check each element for numeric value, and if it is numeric, add it to the total sum. It should also handle cases where tuples contain other tuples, ensuring that the recursion continues until all elements are processed.  

******  

<<with my>>  
The function should be efficient, avoiding unnecessary computations by skipping non-numeric elements. It should use a helper function to check if an element is numeric, and if so, add it to the sum. The function should be able to handle both simple and deeply nested tuples, ensuring that all elements are processed regardless of their position in the structure. It should also return the sum as a single integer value, without any additional formatting.

<<cute dog>>  
The function must be able to process tuples with mixed data types, including non-numeric elements. It should use a loop to iterate through each element of the tuples, and for each element, determine if it is numeric. If it is, the function should add it to the total sum. The function should also handle nested tuples by recursively calling itself on each sub-tuple. The final result should be a single integer that represents the sum of all numeric values in the input structure.

It should be designed to work with any structure that can be represented as a tuple, including nested tuples and lists. The function should be able to process tuples of varying depths and sizes, ensuring that all elements are evaluated for numeric value. It should also be able to handle edge cases, such as empty tuples or tuples with no numeric elements.
